$schema_note: Pass-2 harvest by MisconceptionAnalyst from common_miss in data/buckets/verb_form.congiuntivo.json. PENDING ratification. Uses families ratified in thread v2; new specifics defined here, reused ones under reuses_existing. Thread: inter_chat/Architecture_MisconceptionAnalyst_registry_harvest.md.

proposed_definition.label: Mood form
proposed_definition.description: Producing the form of the wrong mood: building the indicative where the subjunctive (or subjunctive-based imperative) is required. The FORMATION counterpart to discrimination.indicative_for_subjunctive_choice (which is the SELECTION error). Distinct from conjugation_class: the class is right, the mood is wrong.

proposed_family_actions.note: Flagged as the pass-2 family question. Subjunctive importance justifies a dedicated family; alternative is paradigm_confusion. Architecture's call (v3).

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Indicative form built where the subjunctive is required
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Producing the indicative ending/auxiliary when the subjunctive is called for: -ano for -ino, indicative 3pl for the subjunctive 3pl, present/imperfect indicative auxiliary for the subjunctive one.
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: FLAGGED PAIR with discrimination.indicative_for_subjunctive_choice: form here, selection there. Also the home for the imperative 'sei! for sii!' and formal-imperative misses (cross-ref from imperativo).
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: One mood-form pattern across present, passato and trapassato subjunctive.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Using one conjugation class's theme vowel/ending on a verb of another class (beyond the existing 3pl -ano/-ono specifics).
examples: prendi (the -are subjunctive ending on an -ere verb)
parlessi (the -ere imperfect-subjunctive vowel on an -are verb, for parlassi)
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Class-vowel borrowing, distinct from the mood-form error.

reuses_existing.note: vena/sona/abba/faressi/dassi: deriving wrongly or regularising the irregular subjunctive stem.
